name: Profed
description_short: ProFed Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving nine counties in northeast Indiana with competitive savings, checking, loans, and digital banking services.
description_long:
ProFed Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union headquartered in Fort Wayne, Indiana, operating under NCUA insurance. The organization has established itself as a trusted community financial institution serving Adams, Allen, DeKalb, Huntington, LaGrange, Noble, Steuben, Wells, and Whitley counties. Membership is accessible to those who live, work, worship, attend school, volunteer, or regularly conduct business in these counties, with a nominal $5 membership fee.

ProFed offers a comprehensive suite of retail financial products including savings accounts (standard savings, youth savings, club accounts, money market plus savings), certificates of deposit with competitive rates (3.50% APY for EZ Saver Certificates at $25 minimum), checking accounts (student, eChecking, simple, and checking plus options), and credit products (personal loans, auto loans, boat and RV loans, home loans, and home equity lines of credit). The credit union provides Visa debit and rewards cards, HSA accounts, IRAs, and features a skip-a-payment program for loan flexibility. Their digital banking platform enables bill pay, check deposit, and account transfers online.

ProFed distinguishes itself through extensive community engagement and financial education. The institution operates 13 branch locations in northeast Indiana and actively promotes financial literacy through free resources for kids, adults, and businesses covering budgeting, investing, and retirement planning. They sponsor community contests, offer referral incentives ($75 for successful referrals), and provide appointment scheduling for in-person or virtual meetings. Their $475 refi program and construction loan offerings address specific homeowner needs, while the Member Auto Center (MAC) program provides preferred dealer access for auto shoppers.

As a credit union, ProFed delivers the typical member-cooperative benefits: lower fees than traditional banks, competitive rates, and community-focused service. However, membership eligibility is geographically restricted to nine specific Indiana counties, which limits accessibility. While the website demonstrates strong product breadth and community commitment, actual member reviews, loan approval rates, and comparative rate data are not visible from the website alone. The organization appears to serve its regional market effectively but remains a local institution without national reach.
services: Savings accounts (standard, youth, club, money market plus)
Certificates of deposit (traditional and EZ Saver certificates)
Checking accounts (student, eChecking, simple, checking plus)
Visa debit cards and Visa rewards cards
Personal loans and quick cash loans
Auto loans, boat and RV loans
Home loans (conventional, USDA, construction, adjustable-rate, renovation)
Home equity loans and lines of credit
Digital banking and bill pay services
pros: Competitive savings rates including 3.50% APY on EZ Saver Certificates with only $25 minimum deposit
13 branch locations across nine northeast Indiana counties with in-person and virtual appointment options
Comprehensive loan products including conventional mortgages, USDA loans, construction loans, and home equity options
Free financial education resources for children, adults, and businesses covering budgeting, investing, and retirement
Skip-a-payment program providing flexibility for borrowers facing temporary financial challenges
Member referral program offering $75 cash incentives for both referrer and new member
Digital banking platform enabling bill pay, mobile check deposit, and account transfers
cons: Membership is restricted to nine specific Indiana counties, limiting eligibility for out-of-state or non-regional applicants
No publicly available information on competitive rate comparisons, approval rates, or member satisfaction metrics
Limited information on credit card rewards structure and specific benefit details despite offering three Visa card tiers
Website lacks transparent pricing for specific loan types or fee schedules for account maintenance
best_for: Northeast Indiana residents (nine-county service area) seeking competitive rates with local, personalized service
Homebuyers and refinancers interested in construction loans, USDA loans, and home equity products
Consumers prioritizing financial education and community engagement alongside banking services
diagnosis: ProFed Credit Union is best suited for residents of nine northeast Indiana counties seeking membership-based banking with competitive rates, extensive branch access, and community focus. The primary caveat is strict geographic eligibility—you must live, work, worship, attend school, volunteer, or regularly conduct business in Adams, Allen, DeKalb, Huntington, LaGrange, Noble, Steuben, Wells, or Whitley counties to qualify for membership.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
